messages.content: I want to check the value in column J and make sure it is not blank. I also want to check the value in column B and make sure it doe not contain the word Request in the text string. If any of the checks are true then it should run the next code

block. Here's the code:

```
If Not IsEmpty(Range("J1")) And InStr(1, Range("B1"), "Request") = 0 Then
    'Run next code block here
End If
```

This code will first check if the value in J1 is not blank using the `IsEmpty` function. If it is not empty, it will move to the next part of the `If` statement.

The `InStr` function is used to check if the word "Request" is present in the string in cell B1. If it is not present (which is indicated by a return value of 0), the `If` statement will evaluate to True and the code block following it will be executed. If the word "Request" is present in B1, the `If` statement will evaluate to False and the code block will be skipped.
The value I am checking in column J could be in any cell. The value in column B must be in the same row of the value J being checked

To check the value in any cell in column J and make sure the corresponding cell in column B does not contain the word "Request", you can use a loop to iterate through each row in the columns and perform the check for each row. Here is the updated code:

```
Dim lastRow As Long
Dim i As Long

lastRow = Cells(Rows.Count, "J").End(xlUp).Row 'Get the last row of column J

For i = 1 To lastRow 'Loop through each row
    If Not IsEmpty(Cells(i, "J")) And InStr(1, Cells(i, "B"), "Request") = 0 Then
        'Run the code block for each row where the condition is true
    End If
Next i
```

This code uses the `lastRow` variable to get the last row in column J. The loop then iterates from row 1 to the last row, checking the value in column J and corresponding value in column B for each row.

The `Cells` function is used to reference cells based on their row and column index. The `i` variable is used to represent the current row index, while "J" and "B" represent the column indices. By using `Cells(i, "J")`, we are checking the cell in column J for the current row, and using `Cells(i, "B")` to check the corresponding cell in column B.

Again, if the conditions in the `If` statement are true for a given row, the code block following it will be executed for that row.
The value I am checking in column J will be a specific cell and not the entire range. The value in column B must be in the same row of the value  of the specific cell in column J being checked

If you only need to check a specific cell in column J and the corresponding cell in column B, you can use the `Offset` function to reference the cell in column B. Here's the updated code:

```
If Not IsEmpty(Range("J3")) And InStr(1, Range("J3").Offset(0, 1), "Request") = 0 Then
    'Run next code block here
End If
```

In this code, we are checking the value in cell J3, and then using the `Offset` function to reference the cell one column to the right (i.e. the corresponding cell in column B). The `Offset(0, 1)` parameter specifies that we want to move 0 rows and 1 column from the original cell J3.

The rest of the code works the same as before: the `IsEmpty` function checks if the value in J3 is blank, and the `InStr` function checks if the word "Request" is present in the value of the cell in B3. If both conditions are false, the code block following the `If` statement will be executed.
it  will be a specific cell any where in column J

If you need to check a specific cell anywhere in column J and the corresponding cell in column B, you can use the `Find` function to locate the cell in column J and then use `Offset` to reference the corresponding cell in column B. Here's the updated code:

```
Dim targetCell As Range
Set targetCell = Range("J:J").Find("specific value", LookIn:=xlValues)

If Not targetCell Is Nothing And InStr(1, targetCell.Offset(0, 1), "Request") = 0 Then
    'Run next code block here
End If
```

In this code, we first use the `Find` function to locate the cell in column J that contains the specific value you want to check. The `Range("J:J")` parameter specifies that we want to search for the value in column J, and `LookIn:=xlValues` tells Excel to search in the cell values rather than the formulas or formats.

If the `Find` function successfully locates the cell, it will be assigned to the `targetCell` variable. We then use `Offset(0, 1)` to reference the corresponding cell in column B. The rest of the code works the same as before: the `IsEmpty` function checks if the value in the cell in column J is blank, and the `InStr` function checks if the word "Request" is present in the value of the cell in column B. If both conditions are false, the code block following the `If` statement will be executed.
If Target.Column = 10 And Target.Value <> "" And InStr(1, targetCell.Offset(0, 1), "Request") = 0 Then  .... this code gives an error

The error you are encountering could be due to several reasons, but one possibility is that you have not defined the `targetCell` variable before using it in the `InStr` function. You can modify the code to check if both `Target` and `targetCell` are not `Nothing` before using them in the `If` statement. Here's an example code:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    Dim targetCell As Range
    
    'Check if the changed cell is in column J
    If Target.Column = 10 And Target.Value <> "" Then
        'Search for the specific value in column J
        Set targetCell = Range("J:J").Find("specific value", LookIn:=xlValues)
        If Not targetCell Is Nothing And Not Target Is Nothing And InStr(1, targetCell.Offset(0, 1), "Request") = 0 Then
            'Run next code block here
        End If
    End If
End Sub
```

In this code, we have added the line `Dim targetCell As Range` to declare the `targetCell` variable at the beginning of the `Worksheet_Change` event. 

To check if `targetCell` is not `Nothing`, we have added the condition `Not targetCell Is Nothing` in the `If` statement. Similarly, we have added the condition `Not Target Is Nothing` to check if the `Target` range is not `Nothing`.

We have also modified the `Find` function to search for the specific value you are looking for in column J. Once again, the `InStr` function checks if the word "Request" is present in the value of the corresponding cell in column B. If both conditions are false, the code block following the `If` statement will be executed.
If Target.Column = 10 And Target.Value <> "" And InStr(1, targetCell.Offset(0, -9), "Request") = 0 Then what is the process of this code

The modified code you have provided checks if the target cell is in column J (column 10), has a non-blank value, and if the corresponding cell in column B (column 2) does not contain the word "Request" in its value. Here's a breakdown of the code:

```
If Target.Column = 10 And Target.Value <> "" And InStr(1, targetCell.Offset(0, -9), "Request") = 0 Then
    'Run next code block here
End If
```

- `Target.Column = 10`: This condition checks if the changed cell is in column J by comparing its column index (given by `Target.Column`) to 10 (which is the column index of column J). If this condition is false, the code block will not be executed.

- `Target.Value <> ""`: This condition checks if the value in the changed cell is not blank. If this condition is false (i.e. the value is blank), the code block will not be executed.

- `InStr(1, targetCell.Offset(0, -9), "Request") = 0`: This condition checks if the word "Request" is not present in the value of the cell in column B that corresponds to the changed cell in column J. 

The `targetCell.Offset(0, -9)` expression is used to reference the cell in column B that is in the same row as the target cell in column J. Since column B is 9 columns to the left of column J, we use `Offset(0, -9)` to move 0 rows and -9 columns from the target cell. 

The `InStr` function then checks if the word "Request" is present in this cell value. If it is not present (which is indicated by a return value of 0), the `If` statement evaluates to true and the code block following it will be executed. 

Overall, this code block checks if the target cell in column J has a non-blank value and if the corresponding cell in column B does not contain the word "Request". If both conditions are met, the code block will be executed.
The error in the following code say Object Required. If Target.Column = 10 And Target.Value <> "" And InStr(1, targetCell.Offset(0, -9), "Request") = 0 Then

The "Object Required" error typically occurs when you try to use an object that has not been set or is set to a value of `Nothing`. 

In your updated code, the error could be caused by the fact that you have not defined the `targetCell` variable before using it in the `InStr` function. You can fix this error by adding the following line of code before the `If` statement:

```
Set targetCell = Range("J:J").Find("specific value", LookIn:=xlValues)
```

This line locates the cell in column J that contains the specific value you want to check, and assigns it to the `targetCell` variable.

Here's the updated code with this line added:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    Dim targetCell As Range
    
    'Check if the changed cell is in column J
    If Target.Column = 10 And Target.Value <> "" Then
        'Locate the cell in column J that contains the specific value
        Set targetCell = Range("J:J").Find("specific value", LookIn:=xlValues)
        
        'Check if the corresponding cell in column B contains the word "Request"
        If Not targetCell Is Nothing And InStr(1, targetCell.Offset(0, -9), "Request") = 0 Then
            'Run the code block here
        End If
    End If
End Sub
```

Make sure to replace "specific value" with the actual value you are looking for in column J. Also, keep in mind that the `Find` function is case-sensitive, so make sure the value you are looking for matches the case of the cell value exactly.

I am not looking for a specifc value in column J. I only need to know if the cell in Column J is not blank and that the corresponding value in Column B of the same row does not contain the word Request in its text string

In that case, you don't need to use the `Find` function to locate a specific value in column J. You can directly check if the cell in column J is not blank and if the corresponding cell in column B of the same row does not contain the word "Request". Here's an updated code:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    'Check if the changed cell is in column J
    If Target.Column = 10 Then
        'Check if the cell in column J is not blank and if the corresponding cell in column B of the same row does not contain the word "Request"
        If Not IsEmpty(Target) And InStr(1, Target.Offset(0, -8).Value, "Request") = 0 Then
            'Run the code block here
        End If
    End If
End Sub
```

In this code, we use the `IsEmpty` function to check if the value in the target cell (i.e. the changed cell in column J) is blank. We then use `Target.Offset(0, -8).Value` to reference the corresponding cell in column B of the same row (column J is 8 columns to the right of column B, so we use `-8` to specify that we want to move leftward by 8 columns). 

Finally, we use the `InStr` function to check if the value in the corresponding cell in column B contains the word "Request". If the word is not present, the `If` statement evaluates to true and the code block following it will be executed.

Note that you can adjust the offset values as needed depending on the layout of your worksheet. In this example, we assumed that column B is to the left of column J.
how can i write the code for if target cell is J2 then do something

If you only want to check the behavior of the code when the target cell is J2, you can modify the code to only run when the target cell address matches J2. Here's an example code:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    'Check if the changed cell is J2
    If Target.Address = "$J$2" Then
        'Run the code block here for J2 only
    End If
End Sub
```

In this code, we use the `Target.Address` property to get the address of the changed cell. The `$` characters are used to make the row and column references absolute.

We then use an `If` statement to check if the `Target.Address` value matches "$J$2". If the condition is true, the code block following the `If` statement will be executed.

You can replace the comment inside the `If` statement with the actual code you want to run for J2. If the target cell is not J2, the code block will not be executed.
